global.title: Tibetan activist dies after self-immolation near UN office in NYC - Reuters
global.summary: A Tibetan activist died after setting himself on fire outside the United Nations in New York in an appeal for Tibetan independence.
global.whyItMatters: This is a sobering reminder of the desperation tied to oppression, identity, and unanswered cries for justice.

global.title: 'Realistic' fake organs offer cheaper, ethical training for surgeons - Reuters
global.summary: Students at King’s College London have developed lifelike artificial organs that could reduce reliance on cadavers and animals in medical training.
global.whyItMatters: This offers a hopeful example of innovation serving human dignity, healing, and ethical care.

global.title: Supreme Court expands executive power, splits on Trump cases - Reuters
global.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court issued major rulings that broaden executive authority while dividing on several Trump-related cases.
global.whyItMatters: Pastors should watch shifts in political power closely, as they shape public trust, social stability, and the moral tone of civic life.

canadian.title: Human rights museum’s Palestine exhibition sets off feud between Canadian politicians - The Art Newspaper
canadian.summary: A Palestine exhibition at the Canadian Museum for Human Rights has sparked political conflict over museum independence, language around Hamas, and public memory.
canadian.whyItMatters: This touches questions of truth-telling, public witness, and how Canadians narrate suffering and justice.

canadian.title: Rising heat, rising bills: One Texas worker's struggle - Reuters
canadian.summary: Reuters’ coverage also highlighted a Toronto heat warning testing the World Cup fan experience as extreme heat affects major public events.
canadian.whyItMatters: Canadian churches may need to think pastorally about heat, hospitality, and care for vulnerable neighbours during summer gatherings.

persecutedChurch.title: First they came for the Mormons - Baptist News Global
persecutedChurch.summary: A dispute over Pentagon faith classifications raised concerns about government overreach into doctrinal questions and equal treatment of religious groups.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Even when traditions differ theologically, pastors should care whenever the state begins deciding religious legitimacy.

persecutedChurch.title: Tibetan activist dies after self-immolation near UN office in NYC - Reuters
persecutedChurch.summary: A Tibetan activist’s death outside the UN has drawn fresh attention to the long struggle over Tibetan freedom and repression.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It is a call to prayer for people whose suffering remains largely unseen by the wider world.

leadership.title: You Want A Joyful Workplace? It’s All About Your Systems - Forbes
leadership.summary: The article argues that trust, joy, and health at work are shaped less by slogans and more by systems, listening, role clarity, and consistent follow-through.
leadership.whyItMatters: For church leadership, this is a useful reminder that culture is built through habits and structures, not just vision statements.

goodNews.title: An update on Hope - Axios
goodNews.summary: An Axios writer shared a cheerful update on 'Hope,' a giant pumpkin now showing promising female flowers ahead of the Iowa State Fair.
goodNews.whyItMatters: A light, human story like this can help balance a heavy news cycle and remind leaders that delight and ordinary hope matter too.
